visual_checks: Look at all clickable/tappable elements — are any smaller than 24x24 CSS pixels? Check navigation links, icon buttons, close buttons, small action links. Inline text links within paragraphs are exempt.
Measure or visually estimate the size of icon-only buttons such as close (X) buttons, hamburger menus, social media icons, and pagination controls
Check footer links, breadcrumb links, and utility navigation for undersized targets that may be difficult to tap on touch devices

Look for targets that are smaller than 24x24 CSS pixels but have sufficient spacing around them — the 24px requirement can be met by the target size plus its unobscured spacing to the nearest adjacent target
Identify groups of small interactive elements placed close together, such as tag clouds, dense toolbars, or compact icon sets, where both size and spacing may be insufficient
pass_conditions: All clickable/tappable targets are at least 24x24 CSS pixels in size, or the target plus its unobscured spacing to the nearest adjacent target totals at least 24 CSS pixels
Icon buttons (close, menu, social icons, pagination) have a minimum touch target area of 24x24 CSS pixels including any padding
Navigation links in menus and toolbars have adequate size or spacing to meet the 24px minimum threshold
Small visual elements use padding, min-width, or min-height to ensure the interactive area meets the 24x24 CSS pixel minimum
Inline text links within sentences or paragraphs are present but are exempt from this requirement and do not affect the pass assessment
fail_conditions: Icon buttons such as close (X), hamburger menu, or action icons are smaller than 24x24 CSS pixels with insufficient spacing to compensate
Small interactive controls in toolbars or dense navigation areas are both undersized (below 24x24 CSS pixels) and placed too close together with insufficient spacing
Pagination controls or social media icon links have a clickable area smaller than 24x24 CSS pixels with no compensating spacing
Footer links or breadcrumb links are rendered as small text with a tap target well below 24x24 CSS pixels and no additional spacing
Custom checkbox or radio button controls have a visible and interactive area smaller than 24x24 CSS pixels
na_conditions: Page contains no interactive targets, or all interactive elements are inline text links within blocks of text (which are exempt from this criterion)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Target sizes could not be measured because elements are dynamically sized or rendered in a way that prevents accurate measurement from screenshots and DOM data
common_mistakes: Forgetting that the 24x24 CSS pixel requirement applies to the interactive area, not just the visible icon — padding counts toward target size
Confusing this criterion (24px minimum at Level AA) with WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size Enhanced (44px at Level AAA)
Not accounting for spacing — a 20x20 pixel target with 4px of clear space on all sides to the nearest adjacent target meets the requirement
Treating inline text links in paragraphs as failures when they are explicitly exempt from this criterion
Overlooking that user-agent-controlled targets (browser default checkboxes, selects) and targets whose size is determined by the user agent are exempt
Setting a large visual button but restricting the clickable area to only the text or icon inside it

examples.pass: All icon buttons in the toolbar are styled with min-width: 24px and min-height: 24px with adequate padding, giving each a tap target of at least 24x24 CSS pixels
examples.fail: A close button (X) in a modal dialog is rendered as a 16x16 pixel icon with no additional padding, and its nearest adjacent target is only 2px away — total size plus spacing is 18px, below the 24px minimum
examples.partial: Most interactive elements meet the 24x24 CSS pixel minimum, but three social media icon links in the footer are 18x18 pixels with only 2px spacing between them

plain_meaning: Pointer targets must be at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, except for the listed exceptions: spacing (a 24px circle centered on the target does not overlap another target), equivalent alternative elsewhere, inline targets in running text, user-agent-controlled size, or essential specific size. This is the Level AA minimum, less strict than the AAA 44px requirement.
scope_applies_to: Buttons, links, and interactive controls with author-controlled sizing
Icon buttons, close buttons, pagination controls, and small tap targets
Custom controls where the author sets the target dimensions
scope_does_not_apply_to: Inline links embedded in running text within a paragraph
Controls whose size is determined by the user agent (unstyled native form controls)
Targets that meet the spacing exception: a 24px diameter circle centered on the target does not intersect any other target
Targets with an equivalent larger alternative elsewhere on the page
Cases where a specific size is essential to the information
borderline_calibration: The threshold is 24 CSS pixels, not 44; do not apply the AAA 44px rule here.
The spacing exception is generous: small targets with enough breathing room around them pass.
Inline links in paragraphs are always exempt.
Only flag when a clickable area is under 24x24 AND no exception applies.
Check centers and surrounding spacing, not just the visible styled box.
